name: Salcedillo, Puente Rojadillo, and Chozo Tonino Trail
name_original: Chozo Tonino desde Salcedillo — circular por el Parque Natural Montaña Palentina
description: Hike the moderate 5.5-mile Chozo Tonino circular trail from Salcedillo, exploring historical sites and diverse landscapes in Montaña Palenti
region_intro: Hiking trails around Brañosera traverse the diverse landscapes of the Montaña Palentina Natural Park, characterized by high mountain scenery and extensive deciduous forests of beech and oak. The region features prominent peaks like Pico Valdecebollas and is shaped by rivers such as the Rubagón and Camesa, which carve through valleys. This varied terrain provides a range of hiking experiences, from forest paths to mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Brañosera The most popular hiking route is Pico Valdecebollas from Golobar Trail, a 3.3 miles (5.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views and leads towards "El Torreón." Another top favourite among local hikers is Salcedillo, Puente Rojadillo, and Chozo Tonino Trail, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.9 km) path. It explores historical sites and diverse landscapes, including a stone cabin and a Roman bridge. Local hikers also love the Pico Valdecebollas and Pozo Merino Loop, a 12.4 miles (20.0 km) trail leading through high mountain scenery, often completed in about 6 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Brañosera is defined by high mountain scenery, extensive deciduous forests, and river valleys, with options for various ability levels from moderate forest paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Brañosera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Brañosera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Brañosera, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: Brañosera offers a wide range of hiking experiences, with over 40 trails available. You'll find options for various abilities, including 11 easy routes, 32 moderate trails, and 2 more challenging hikes, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique natural features to discover on hikes near Brañosera?
A: Yes, the area is rich in natural beauty. A notable feature is &lt;strong&gt;Pozo Merino&lt;/strong&gt;, a beautiful waterfall fed by the Rubagón river. In winter, it can even form impressive ice waterfalls. The region also boasts extensive deciduous forests of beech and oak, and rivers like the Camesa, which carves through the landscape, creating features such as the historic Puente Rojadillo bridge.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on the hiking trails in Brañosera?
A: Hikers in Brañosera can enjoy diverse and striking views. Many trails offer panoramic vistas of the &lt;strong&gt;Montaña Palentina Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, especially from higher elevations like &lt;strong&gt;Pico Valdecebollas&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll also encounter scenic forest paths, river valleys, and picturesque mountain scenery throughout the region. For specific viewpoints, consider exploring highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/586766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Piedrasluengas Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Brañosera?
A: While the guide doesn't specifically label routes as 'family-friendly,' the presence of 11 easy trails suggests suitable options for families. These routes typically have minimal elevation gain and cover shorter distances, allowing for a pleasant outing with children. The diverse forests and riverbanks provide engaging natural environments for younger hikers to explore.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails around Brañosera?
A: The Montaña Palentina Natural Park, where Brañosera is located, is generally welcoming to dogs, though specific regulations may apply in certain protected areas or during wildlife breeding seasons. It's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the park's rich biodiversity, which includes species like the Cantabrian brown bear and Iberian wolf. Most trails are suitable for dogs, but always ensure they are well-behaved and you clean up after them.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Brañosera?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. For instance, the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104356494/pico-valdecebollas-and-pozo-merino-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Valdecebollas and Pozo Merino Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views over 12.4 miles. A moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103997582/salcedillo-puente-rojadillo-and-chozo-tonino-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salcedillo, Puente Rojadillo, and Chozo Tonino Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which explores historical sites and diverse landscapes. Another moderate circular trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093126490/gr-1-historical-trail-perapertu-and-el-portillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 1 Historical Trail: Perapertú and El Portillo Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Brañosera?
A: Brañosera offers hiking opportunities throughout the year, each season providing a unique experience. Spring and autumn are particularly popular due to mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors – spring brings lush greenery and wildflowers, while autumn transforms the deciduous forests into a spectacle of reds and golds. Summer is ideal for higher altitude hikes, and winter can be magical with snow-covered landscapes, though some routes may require specialized gear or experience due to ice and snow, especially around Pozo Merino.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see while hiking in Brañosera?
A: Yes, the region has a rich history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103997582/salcedillo-puente-rojadillo-and-chozo-tonino-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salcedillo, Puente Rojadillo, and Chozo Tonino Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, takes you past a Roman bridge over the Camesa River and a traditional stone cabin. Brañosera itself is considered the first municipality in Spain, adding to its historical significance. The area is also part of the long-distance &lt;strong&gt;GR 1 Historical Trail&lt;/strong&gt;, which traverses Spain through spectacular mountain scenery.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Brañosera?
A: The trails in Brañosera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, from high mountain scenery to extensive deciduous forests, and the well-marked paths. The panoramic views from peaks like &lt;strong&gt;Pico Valdecebollas&lt;/strong&gt; and the tranquility of the natural park are also commonly highlighted as favorite aspects.

Q: Are there any specific peaks or summits I can hike to in the Brañosera area?
A: Absolutely. The most prominent peak in the area is &lt;strong&gt;Pico Valdecebollas&lt;/strong&gt;, standing at 2,139 meters. It offers rewarding experiences and panoramic views. You can reach its summit via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093102700/pico-valdecebollas-from-golobar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Valdecebollas from Golobar Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable summits in the broader region include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Mares Peak&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Labra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Brañosera, or is parking readily available?
A: While specific public transport options directly to all trailheads might be limited in this natural park setting, Brañosera itself is accessible. For those driving, parking is generally available in and around the village, serving as a convenient starting point for many routes. It's advisable to check local information for specific trailhead parking facilities, especially during peak seasons.
tips: Small stone-built cabin hidden in a beech forest and said to have been used by a maqui in exile. You can sit down and try to imagine the hard life that the outlawed guerrillas of the Franco regime had to go through.

Town of the Palencia Mountain at the foot of the Sierra de la Braña. It is one of the highest towns in the province, being more than 1200 meters.
It is a pretty town where you can make a stop if you are in the area and visit the Romanesque church of San Martín Obispo.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Chozo Tonino circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins in the village of Salcedillo. You should find parking available within the village, which serves as the primary trailhead for this route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows pistas forestales (forest tracks) and antiguos caminos (old paths). While generally well-defined, sections can become somewhat slippery after rain. You'll traverse diverse environments, including oak groves, heathlands, and pastures.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through pastures used for cattle and is located within the Natural Park Montaña Palentina, it's advisable to keep your dog on a leash to ensure the safety of wildlife and livestock.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers several notable points of interest. You'll encounter the historic Chozo Tonino, a traditional shepherd's hut with a rich history. The route also crosses the picturesque Puente Rojadillo, an excellent historical bridge. Additionally, you'll pass through Monte Mayor and may spot historical vestiges of the traditional millstone making industry.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina region is beautiful throughout the year, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring snow, requiring appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for the Natural Park Montaña Palentina?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits required or entrance fees to access the Natural Park Montaña Palentina for hiking. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning wildlife and environmental protection.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I see in the Montaña Palentina Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The Montaña Palentina is known for its diverse wildlife. Hikers on this trail might spot Iberian red deer and various bird species. Keep an eye out, especially in quieter sections of the route.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, part of the return route to Salcedillo follows sections of the GR1, also known as the Historic Trail, which is well-signposted. You will also intersect with parts of the Asturian Way and the Sendero Histórico: Palencia.

faqs.question: Is the Chozo Tonino circular trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: With its moderate difficulty, manageable length of 8.8 kilometers, and historical points of interest, this trail can be suitable for families with children who are accustomed to hiking. Some uphill sections provide a good workout, but the paths are generally accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead in Salcedillo?
faqs.answer: Salcedillo is a small village, so facilities might be limited directly at the trailhead. It's advisable to check in advance for local cafes or accommodation options in Salcedillo or nearby larger towns within the Montaña Palentina region if you plan to stay overnight or need refreshments after your hike.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, we recommend bringing sturdy hiking boots, sufficient water, snacks, and appropriate clothing layers for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also advisable, especially for navigating forest tracks and old paths.

waypoints.name: Church of San Martín Obispo de Salcedillo
